name: Proportional-Integral (PI) Negative-Feedback Controller
description: A continuous-time ODE-inspired controller where the agent maintains an internal energy variable via proportional and integral error correction. The error signal (deviation from set point) drives both a proportional term for immediate response and an integral term for accumulated-error memory. Actions are selected by computing utility scores that combine the total control signal with proximity to food resources. Grounded in Gross et al. (2024), Drengstig et al. (2012), and npj Digital Medicine (2020) PI models of homeostatic control. Implements postulates P1 (set point maintenance), P2 (error-proportional correction), and P3 (negative feedback with sensor/comparator/effector).

variables.name: energy
variables.description: Internal energy level of the agent (regulated variable)

variables.name: set_point
variables.description: Target energy level the agent seeks to maintain

variables.name: error_signal
variables.description: Deviation from set point: e = s - A

variables.name: proportional_control
variables.description: Instantaneous corrective drive proportional to error

variables.name: integral_control
variables.description: Accumulated error over time (integral memory with windup clamp)

variables.name: ate_last_tick
variables.description: Whether the agent successfully ate food on the previous tick

parameters.name: proportional_gain
parameters.source: Tuned; consistent with PI models in npj Digital Medicine (2020)

parameters.name: integral_gain
parameters.source: Tuned; lower than k_P to prevent oscillation per Drengstig et al. (2012)

parameters.name: passive_energy_decay
parameters.source: Models basal metabolic cost; Gross et al. (2024)

parameters.name: energy_per_eat
parameters.source: Tuned to interaction with decay rate d

parameters.name: integral_windup_cap
parameters.source: Standard anti-windup engineering practice; Drengstig et al. (2012)

rules.description: Error signal computation: deviation of energy from set point
Proportional control term: immediate response proportional to error
Integral control term update: accumulated error with anti-windup clamping
Total control signal: sum of proportional and integral terms
Energy dynamics: passive decay plus food intake
rules.pseudocode: A = clamp(A - d + Delta_eat * (1 if ate else 0), 0, A_max)

rules.description: Eat utility: control signal weighted by food presence at current position
Move utility: control signal weighted by inverse Manhattan distance to nearest food from candidate cell
Stay utility: small negative value proportional to error magnitude (penalty for inaction when energy is off set point)

decision_logic.description: PI controller-driven action selection. The agent computes the error signal and PI control terms, then uses the total control signal to weight action utilities. When energy is at or above set point, the agent rests. When below, it forages by eating if food is present, moving toward the nearest food if visible, or exploring randomly if no food is visible.
pseudocode: # Update internal state from last action
A = clamp(A - d + Delta_eat * (1 if ate else 0), 0, A_max)
food_at_position = any food with (food.x == position.x and food.y == position.y)
# Energy at or above set point; rest
# Food here and we need energy; eat
# Food visible; move toward nearest food
if p_prime out of grid bounds: continue
dist = min Manhattan distance from p_prime to any food

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_position: any f in perception.resources.food where f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y
perception_to_variables.ate_food: perception.last_action_result — check if eat action was successful (consumed == true or reward > 0)

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; internally the agent tracks its own energy A(t) and uses the PI control signal, not the environment reward, as its primary decision driver

expected_behaviors.description: Energy decays without eating, causing error signal to grow and control signal to increase
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 20 steps with no food on grid → assert A decreases each step, e increases each step, c increases each step

expected_behaviors.description: Agent actively forages when energy is below set point
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place food on grid, start agent at A=40 → agent should move toward and eat food within reasonable steps

expected_behaviors.description: Agent rests when energy is at or above set point (satiation and cessation)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set A=85 (above s=80) → agent selects 'stay' action

expected_behaviors.description: Integral term accumulates during prolonged deprivation, increasing urgency over time
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 50 steps below set point → c_I should be significantly positive and larger than at step 10

expected_behaviors.description: Integral windup cap prevents runaway control signal
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 200 steps with no food → assert abs(c_I) <= c_I_max at all times

references: Gross, F. et al. (2024) — A Functional Approach to Homeostatic Regulation — Biology Direct / bioRxiv
Drengstig, T. et al. (2012) — A Basic Set of Homeostatic Controller Motifs — PMC3491718
npj Digital Medicine (2020) — Homeostasis as a Proportional–Integral Control System — Nature
Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2011) — A Reinforcement Learning Theory for Homeostatic Regulation — NeurIPS
Cannon, W.B. (1932) — The Wisdom of the Body
Bernard, C. (1865) — Introduction à l'étude de la médecine expérimentale
